behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is at the boundary between manifest validation and plugin loading/execution order. A pure Unit test of `validateManifest` can miss regressions where the loader imports the plugin module before enforcing validation. Using a temp plugin directory with an entry module side effect verifies rejection happens before any plugin code runs. Higher levels were considered and rejected: behavior text is limited to local plugin loading from a configured directory (no real network/OS primitive beyond normal local filesystem, no AI).
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugins directory with one plugin whose `manifest.json` omits a required field and whose entry module writes a sentinel file or mutates a global on import. Run the loader end-to-end in-process. Assert: loader reports manifest rejection, `onLoad` is never called, and sentinel side effect is absent.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's `onLoad` hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when dependency resolution and hook invocation are wired together; this is module handoff, not one-function logic. A Unit test of topological sort alone can pass while loader invocation order is wrong. Integration with two real plugin modules catches the ordering regression. Higher levels rejected: behavior only concerns local dependency ordering and hook call sequence, with no subprocess/network/AI.
behaviors.test_description: Set up temp directory with plugins A and B where A depends on B. Each `onLoad` appends its name to a shared in-memory log/file. Run loader and assert call order is `[B, A]`.

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection must be validated at the resolver→executor boundary: not just detecting the cycle, but preventing both plugins from loading. Unit graph tests can miss accidental execution despite detection. Integration test with two plugin packages verifies both error reporting and zero loads. Higher levels rejected: behavior text does not require real subprocess/network/external API or AI output.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins A and B with mutual dependencies and observable `onLoad` side effects. Execute loader. Assert: cycle error includes A/B relationship, neither plugin entry side effect nor `onLoad` side effect occurs.

behaviors.description: A plugin's `onLoad` hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering contract between module import execution and lifecycle invocation. A Unit test of hook dispatcher cannot prove import side effects happened first. Integration with a real plugin entry module side effect is the minimum to expose race/order bugs. System was considered and rejected because behavior text only requires in-process module import semantics in local runtime, not subprocess/real network.
behaviors.test_description: Build a plugin whose entry module sets `globalThis.pluginInitialized = true` on import; `onLoad` throws if that flag is false. Run loader and assert no throw from ordering violation, and verify `onLoad` executed after import side effect.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its `onLoad` hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is orchestration/state propagation: error handling plus dependency-aware continuation across multiple plugins. Unit tests of try/catch or dependency checks alone can miss incorrect stop-the-world behavior. Integration with a small plugin graph verifies continuation policy and dependent-skip behavior together. Higher levels rejected: behavior is local loader control flow only; no subprocess/network/AI and no multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins: `bad` throws in `onLoad`; `independent` has no dependency on `bad`; `dependent` depends on `bad`. Run loader. Assert: `bad` failure is recorded, `independent` still loads, and `dependent` is not initialized due to failed dependency.
